section_miscategorizations.reason: This appears to be a patient-reported home blood glucose range rather than an in-clinic lab or vital; it fits better as history but does not substantially mislead as an objective finding.
Absence of fever is being reported by the patient as part of the symptom history rather than documented as an exam finding.
This duplicates the subsequent statement "The patient has diabetes." within the same section; while not a cross-section error, it is an organizational issue but does not change clinical meaning.
Redundant with the prior more specific diagnosis of type 2 diabetes; this is an internal organizational issue within the section rather than a true miscategorization.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is currently taking metformin 500 milligrams twice a day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Current medication regimen belongs in history/medication history; placing it in the plan could mislead a reader into thinking this is a new or changed order.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is currently taking flovent twice a day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is existing therapy, not a new intervention; documenting it in the plan may be misread as a new prescription rather than current medication state.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is currently taking albuterol as needed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is part of the current medication regimen; in the plan section it may be interpreted as a new or adjusted order.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is currently taking metformin 500 milligrams once a day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is another statement of current medication use; listing it under plan risks confusion about whether this is a new dosing instruction versus historical information.
